key_insight: A frozen CT-CLIP foundation model, paired with a lightweight survival head, can improve lung cancer survival prediction from pretreatment CT and clinical data in a small cohort.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help risk-stratify patients more accurately than clinical variables alone, supporting treatment planning when labeled imaging data are limited.
method: Used CT-CLIP as a feature extractor for pretreatment CT images and clinical variables.
Compared frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, and low-rank adaptation (LoRA).
Tested modality ablations against clinical and multimodal baselines for survival prediction.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 242 diagnosed lung cancer patients, with the frozen CT-CLIP plus trainable survival head outperforming the clinical baseline and matching or exceeding other multimodal approaches.
watch_item: The cohort is small and from a data-constrained setting, so external validation is needed before broad clinical use.
paper_title: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
